sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Loom connector** integrates with Loom workspaces via the Loom API v1. It enables Shielva to sync videos (with transcripts), folders, and workspaces in real-time.

This connector uses **Bearer token** authentication — an API key generated from Loom Account Settings → Integrations → API.

**Key capabilities:**
- List all videos via `GET /videos` with `next_page` cursor pagination
- Fetch individual video metadata via `GET /videos/{id}`
- Retrieve video transcripts via `GET /videos/{id}/transcript` (falls back to description if unavailable)
- List folder hierarchy via `GET /folders`
- List workspaces via `GET /workspaces`
- Stable document IDs via sha256("video:"+id)[:16] for deduplication across syncs
- Exponential-backoff retry (max 3 attempts) — skips retry on AuthError and NotFoundError

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates api_key presence; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE/MISSING_CREDENTIALS |
| `health_check()` | Calls `GET /me` to verify API key; returns user name/email in message |
| `sync()` | Paginates all videos, fetches transcripts best-effort, normalizes folders + workspaces |
| `list_videos()` | Paginated `GET /videos` — follows next_page cursor until exhausted |
| `list_folders(folder_id?)` | `GET /folders` or `GET /folders/{id}` |
| `list_workspaces()` | `GET /workspaces` |
| `get_video(video_id)` | `GET /videos/{id}` for a single video |
| `get_video_transcript(video_id)` | `GET /videos/{id}/transcript` — empty dict if unavailable |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: The connector uses **Bearer token** authentication — a Loom API key sent in the `Authorization` header on every request.

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `api_key` — generate at Loom → Account Settings → Integrations → API.

children.title: Auth Header
children.content: `Authorization: Bearer {api_key}` — set on every HTTP request.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| API Key | `api_key` | password | Yes | Loom API key from Account Settings → Integrations → API |

sections.title: Resources Synced
sections.content: The connector syncs three resource types from the Loom API.

children.title: Videos
children.content:
**Endpoint:** `GET /videos` (paginated via `next_page` cursor)

Each video document includes:
- Title and description
- Share URL and status (processing / transcoding / ready / error)
- Duration (seconds)
- Folder ID and Workspace ID
- Created and updated timestamps
- **Transcript** (when available) as the primary content body

**Stable ID:** `sha256("video:" + id)[:16]`
**Document type:** `loom_video`

children.title: Folders
children.content:
**Endpoint:** `GET /folders` or `GET /folders/{id}`

Each folder document includes:
- Folder name
- Parent folder ID (if nested)
- Workspace ID
- Created timestamp

**Stable ID:** `sha256("folder:" + id)[:16]`
**Document type:** `loom_folder`

children.title: Workspaces
children.content:
**Endpoint:** `GET /workspaces`

Each workspace document includes:
- Workspace name
- Member count
- Created timestamp

**Stable ID:** `sha256("workspace:" + id)[:16]`
**Document type:** `loom_workspace`

sections.title: Pagination
sections.content:
The Loom API uses **cursor-based pagination** via the `next_page` token.

The connector follows this pattern:
1. Initial request: `GET /videos?limit=50`
2. If response includes `next_page`, pass it as `?next_page={cursor}` on the next call
3. Continue until `next_page` is absent or null

All pages are accumulated into a single result list before normalization.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
The connector maps Loom API HTTP status codes to typed exceptions:

| Status | Exception | Retry? |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| 401 | `LoomAuthError` | No — re-auth required |
| 403 | `LoomAuthError` | No — permission denied |
| 404 | `LoomNotFoundError` | No — resource absent |
| 429 | `LoomRateLimitError` | Yes (up to 3 attempts, exponential backoff) |
| 5xx | `LoomNetworkError` | Yes (up to 3 attempts, exponential backoff) |
| Network | `LoomNetworkError` | Yes (up to 3 attempts, exponential backoff) |

Transcript fetch errors are **best-effort** — a `LoomNotFoundError` on transcript simply means the connector uses the video description as content instead of crashing the sync.

sections.title: Stable Document IDs
sections.content:
Every synced document receives a **stable 16-character ID** computed as:

```
sha256("<resource_type>:<resource_id>")[:16]
```

Examples:
- Video: `sha256("video:vid-abc123")[:16]`
- Folder: `sha256("folder:folder-xyz")[:16]`
- Workspace: `sha256("workspace:ws-123")[:16]`

This ensures the same Loom resource always maps to the same Shielva document ID, enabling safe incremental syncs without duplication.

sections.title: Rate Limits
sections.content:
Loom enforces API rate limits per key:

| Plan | Limit |
|------|-------|
| Standard | ~60 requests/minute |
| Enterprise | Higher limits (contact Loom) |

The connector automatically retries on `429` with exponential backoff (1s → 2s → 4s). For workspaces with large video libraries, schedule syncs during off-peak hours.
